name: resource

metadata.name: Resource
metadata.summary: A Python library concentrated on the Resource layer of RESTful APIs.

documentation: A Python library concentrated on the Resource layer of RESTful APIs.

functions.name: rsrc.framework.flask.payload
documentation:
Performs sanity checks or decoding depending on the Content-Type,
then returns the request payload as a dict. If request Content-Type is
unsupported, aborts with a 400 (Bad Request).

Borrowed from `eve` - https://github.com/nicolaiarocci/eve

classes.name: rsrc.framework.view.ProxyView
documentation:
Delegate requests from framework-view to resource-view.

Subclasses of `ProxyView` should set the `view` attribute, and override
the following methods:
    get_uri
    get_query_params
    get_auth_params
    get_data
    make_response

functions.name: rsrc.utils.import_object
documentation:
Imports an object by name.

import_object('x') is equivalent to 'import x'.
import_object('x.y.z') is equivalent to 'from x.y import z'.

```pycon
>>> import tornado.escape
>>> import_object('tornado.escape') is tornado.escape
True
>>> import_object('tornado.escape.utf8') is tornado.escape.utf8
True
>>> import_object('tornado') is tornado
True
>>> import_object('tornado.missing_module')
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
ImportError: No module named missing_module
```

Borrowed from `tornado` - <https://github.com/tornadoweb/tornado>

functions.name: rsrc.utils.normalize_uri
documentation:
Normalize `uri` according to `settings.TRAILING_SLASH`.

settings.TRAILING_SLASH | uri               | normalized
----------------------- | ----------------- | -----------------
True                    | '/'               | '/'
True                    | 'www.github.com'  | 'www.github.com/'
True                    | 'www.github.com/' | 'www.github.com/'
False                   | '/'               | '/'
False                   | 'www.github.com'  | 'www.github.com'
False                   | 'www.github.com/' | 'www.github.com'

functions.name: rsrc.filter.query_params

classes.name: rsrc.filter.Filter

methods.name: rsrc.filter.Filter.merge
documentation: Merge `conditions` using AND-logic into one condition.

The Class for complex filtering.

Except for simple filtering by query parameters, two more complex
filtering schemes are to be supported:

1. user-defined methods (supported now)

    a. override `query` method
    b. add `query_<name>` method

    All methods should return MongoDB-style conditions.

2. `filter` keyword in query string (not supported yet)

    for example:
    <resource>/?filter=name==russell and age>=int(10) and date_joined<datetime(2014-10-10T11:40:00Z)

    Value string of `filter` will be parsed as MongoDB-style conditions.

modules.name: rsrc.exceptions
documentation:
Handled exceptions raised by Resources.

Inspired by `Django REST framework` - https://github.com/tomchristie/django-rest-framework

classes.name: rsrc.exceptions.StatusCodeError
documentation:
Base class for kinds of Status-Code Resource exceptions.
Subclasses should provide `.status_code` and `.default_detail` properties.

classes.name: rsrc.contrib.token.user.TokenUser

methods.name: rsrc.contrib.token.user.TokenUser.get_key
documentation:
Get token-key of the user that matches `username` and `password`.

Must return a tuple (pk, secret).

Should be overridden with custom logic.

methods.name: rsrc.contrib.token.user.TokenUser.exists
documentation:
Check if `pk` and `secret` match an existing user.

Should be overridden with custom logic.

methods.name: rsrc.contrib.token.user.TokenUser.invalidate_key
documentation:
Invalidate the key related to `pk`.

In recommended implementation, this means:
    + Find an user that matches `pk` as his `id`, and change his
    `jwt_secret`, and return True.
    + If `pk` matches no user, return False

Should be overridden with custom logic.

Handle the `token-key` of the user.

`token-key` is token (JWT) related data stored in user model.

Below is the structure of `token-key`:

    token-key               | user model
    ----------------------- | ----------
    pk (primary key)        | id
    secret (JWT secret key) | jwt_secret

`jwt_secret` is a recommended column name. Of course, you can use your
preferred name.

classes.name: rsrc.contrib.token.auth.TokenAuth
documentation:
Token-based authentication.

The Authorization header will contain the auth token as the username.

classes.name: rsrc.contrib.token.view.TokenView

methods.name: rsrc.contrib.token.view.TokenView.post
documentation: Make a token based on `uesrname`, `password` and `expires`.

methods.name: rsrc.contrib.token.view.TokenView.delete
documentation: Invalidate the token related to the user matches `pk`.

Generate tokens based on uesrname and password.

`Login` in RESTful API:
    1. POST (with username and password) to generate a token
    2. get the token from the response (in JSON format) of POST
    3. use `TokenAuth` to protect your resources at server side,
    and the client should set the token as username part in the
    Authorization header to access resources
    4. for browser users, save the token in cookies of their browsers,
    and get the token from cookies to set Authorization header every time

methods.name: rsrc.contrib.dufilter.DuFilter.get_du_params
rsrc.auth.BasicAuth.make_headers
documentation: The response headers when authentication fails.

methods.name: rsrc.auth.BasicAuth.authenticated
documentation:
Authenticate the user with credentials in `auth_params`.

Sometimes, `method` also affect the authentication here.

methods.name: rsrc.auth.BasicAuth.authorized
documentation: Validate if the authenticated user has required permissions.

modules.name: rsrc.status

Descriptive HTTP status codes, for code readability.
